extraction_completeness.assessment: PASS - Excellent recall (99.5%), only 2 minor terms missed across 397 bold patterns

tier_accuracy.assessment: PASS - All 30 sampled terms correctly tiered. Critical terms meet 3+ articles / 2+ domains / 30+ words criteria. Supporting terms correctly lack substantial definitions.

definition_quality.note: All flagged terms are supporting-tier with empty definitions. This is systemic (1130/8266 = 13.7% empty defs), affecting only supporting-tier terms that are section headers without substantive content. Among non-supporting terms, pass rate is much higher.
definition_quality.assessment: CONDITIONAL PASS - Empty definitions are a known systemic issue limited to supporting-tier terms. All important/critical terms in sample had adequate definitions.

potential_false_stops.note: Very broad but has specific technical content
Broad but some entries are specific chemical preparations

stop_word_handling.assessment: PASS - Stop words correctly tiered as supporting or important-with-override. No domain-specific technical terms incorrectly stopped. Some stop words (cooling, drying, storage) have accumulated enough technical context to warrant reconsideration, but current tiering is defensible.

fixes_applied_to: Number of theoretical plates: process -> noun
type_classification.assessment: PASS after fixes - 22 type corrections applied. Pre-fix sample showed 100% accuracy on the 20 random samples, but targeted review of known misclassified terms found 22 errors. Type distribution is heavily skewed: noun=5934 (72%), material=1603 (19%), equipment=694 (8%), process=35 (0.4%). Process type is dramatically undercounted.

top_missing.context: Core distillation concept, mentioned multiple times without bold
Key concept in distillation and metallurgy
Key operating parameter (bold in later section but as 'Minimum reflux ratio')
Major iron product (present but many related terms missing)
Key steel property parameter (present but many related missing)

coverage_gaps.assessment: EXPECTED - Bold-pattern extraction captures section headers but misses inline technical terms. The 20 most significant gaps are fundamental concepts (azeotrope, bloomery, blast furnace, wrought iron) that would benefit from NLP extraction or manual addition.

systemic_issues.title: Process type dramatically undercounted
systemic_issues.description: Only 35 terms classified as 'process' out of 8,266 (0.4%). Many process terms are classified as 'noun' or 'equipment'. The type_classification heuristics in rating-config.yaml rely on pattern matching in definitions, but definitions extracted from bold patterns often don't contain the trigger phrases (e.g., 'Annealing' definition doesn't say 'process of'). 22 type fixes were applied, but this is a small fraction of the likely misclassified terms.
systemic_issues.recommendation: Add suffix-based heuristic: terms ending in -ing, -tion, -ment, -ure that describe actions should be auto-classified as process. Re-run classification with expanded patterns.

systemic_issues.title: 13.7% empty definitions (1,130 terms)
systemic_issues.description: 1,130 out of 8,266 terms have empty definitions. All are supporting-tier terms that were extracted from bold section headers but had no inline definition. These are effectively just index entries without content.
systemic_issues.recommendation: Either (a) mark these explicitly as 'header-only' in a new field, or (b) set definition to a brief auto-generated summary from surrounding context. The empty definitions don't harm the glossary but reduce its utility as a reference.

systemic_issues.title: Generic header terms inflated to critical tier
systemic_issues.description: Terms like 'Advantages', 'Materials', 'Process', 'Principle', 'Properties', 'Construction' are generic section headers that appear across many articles and domains. They meet the critical-tier criteria mechanically (3+ articles, 2+ domains, 30+ words) but are not meaningful tech-tree nodes. 'Advantages' appears in 29 articles across 13 domains - it's a document structure pattern, not a technical term.
systemic_issues.recommendation: Add a 'generic header' detection rule to the rating pipeline that prevents section-header-like terms from reaching critical tier regardless of article count.

systemic_issues.title: Bold-pattern extraction misses inline technical terms
systemic_issues.description: Coverage gap analysis shows fundamental concepts (azeotrope, bloomery, blast furnace, wrought iron) are missing because they appear in running text without bold markup. The extraction only captures **term**: definition patterns.
systemic_issues.recommendation: Complement bold-pattern extraction with NLP-based term extraction that identifies technical terms by frequency, domain specificity, and contextual importance.

systemic_issues.title: Extraction artifacts: markdown links as term names
systemic_issues.description: 30 terms had markdown link syntax as their term name (e.g., '[Energy → Charcoal](../energy/charcoal.md)'). These were bold-pattern extractions from cross-reference sections. Fixed to clean names, but the root cause is the extraction pipeline not stripping markdown link syntax.
systemic_issues.recommendation: Add markdown link sanitization step to extraction pipeline: strip link syntax, keep display text only.
